description_long: This shotgun features a compact, tactical design with a robust metallic body and a prominent rectangular barrel shroud. The main body is primarily dark gray metal, with a cylindrical brown component running parallel to the barrel, likely serving as a pump or fore-end. The trigger area is enclosed by a large, rounded guard with two distinct finger holes, providing ergonomic grip and control. The top of the shotgun displays mechanical details such as rails and mounting points, suggesting compatibility with attachments. The rear section is blocky and lacks a traditional stock, instead ending abruptly with a squared-off back. Overall, the weapon has a modern, utilitarian appearance suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, tactical shotgun with a dark metallic body, brown cylindrical fore-end, and a distinctive dual-finger trigger guard. The design is modern and robust, lacking a traditional stock.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular barrel opening and the symmetrical, blocky structure of the shotgun's muzzle.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the brown cylindrical pump, dual-finger trigger guard, and mechanical details along the top rail.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the squared-off back, absence of a traditional stock, and the alignment of the barrel and pump.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the pump, trigger guard, and top rail details from the other side.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, rectangular shroud with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: elongated, rounded trigger guard with two large finger holes and a reinforced structure
